wiki-reindex.description: Full reindex of the Obsidian vault: rebuilds tag indexes (wiki/tags/), consolidated tag index (wiki/tags-index.md), link graph (wiki/data/links-graph.json), graph analysis (wiki/data/graph-stats.md), and MOC index (wiki/moc-index.md). Safe — only writes to wiki/ artifacts, never touches vault notes.
wiki-reindex.template: Load the llm-wiki skill. Run: python3 wiki/scripts/index-tags.py && python3 wiki/scripts/generate-tags-index.py && python3 wiki/scripts/build-links-graph.py && python3 wiki/scripts/graph-analyze.py && python3 wiki/scripts/generate-moc-index.py && python3 wiki/scripts/index-dates.py. After all scripts complete, read tag index files in wiki/tags/. For any index file MISSING a description field in its frontmatter, generate a one-sentence Russian description based on the note titles with that tag. Do NOT overwrite existing descriptions. Limit to 30 descriptions per run (see wiki/config.json). Re-run generate-tags-index.py to update the consolidated index. Read monthly date index files in wiki/dates/. For any monthly file MISSING a description, generate a short Russian summary (1-2 sentences). Do NOT overwrite existing descriptions. Read wiki/data/graph-stats.md and wiki/moc-index.md. Report hub rankings, cluster sizes, and top tags to user. Log to wiki/LOG.md.

wiki-research.description: Research a given topic using the vault. Searches tag indices in wiki/tags/ for relevant notes and compiles findings. Read-only on vault.
wiki-research.template: Load the llm-wiki skill. Read wiki/tags-index.md. Find relevant tags by keyword match on tag names and descriptions (max 10 tags). For each relevant tag, read wiki/tags/<tag>.md to discover connected notes. Read the actual notes (max 50 total). Compile a structured research summary in Russian: Краткий снимок → Ключевые находки → Выводы → Источники. Save to wiki/research/<YYYYMMDDHHMM> Topic.md with frontmatter: topic, date, tags (tags used). Return summary to user. Log to wiki/LOG.md.

wiki-analyze.description: Analyze the vault for new Zettelkasten connections. Runs graph + TF-IDF analysis, generates link suggestions, validates them by reading note content, then SHOWS A PREVIEW and WAITS for user confirmation before applying any links to notes.
wiki-analyze.template: Load the llm-wiki skill. Step 1 — generate suggestions: python3 wiki/scripts/build-links-graph.py && python3 wiki/scripts/graph-suggest-links.py && python3 wiki/scripts/tfidf-suggest.py. Step 2 — read wiki/data/link-suggestions.md and wiki/data/semantic-suggestions.md. Step 3 — FILTER: exclude pairs where both notes are from daily notes/, exclude pairs where either note has ONLY tags listed in wiki/config.json exclude.tags_from_analyze, exclude graph pairs with jaccard=0 and no shared_tags, exclude semantic pairs with similarity < min_tfidf_similarity (config). Step 4 — take top 20 from filtered list. Step 5 — VALIDATE (read-only): read both notes for each suggestion, assess semantic relevance, classify each as accepted/rejected with a one-sentence reason in Russian. Step 6 — PREVIEW: show the user a table of accepted suggestions with columns: Источник, Цель, Тип (graph/tfidf), Причина. WAIT for explicit user confirmation. Step 7 — only after user says 'yes' or 'да' or 'применить': for each accepted suggestion, add the target to the source note's links: frontmatter field (append to existing list, dedup; if field absent, create it as YAML list; if string, convert to list). Log each edit immediately. Step 8 — re-run: python3 wiki/scripts/build-links-graph.py && python3 wiki/scripts/graph-analyze.py. Step 9 — log to wiki/LOG.md with accepted/rejected counts.

wiki-lint.description: Read-only health check of vault and skill artifacts. Checks stale tag indexes, missing descriptions, broken wikilinks, graph orphans, artifact consistency, orphan MOC notes, malformed links.
wiki-lint.template: Load the llm-wiki skill. Run a read-only health check. 1) Stale tag indexes: compare wiki/tags/*.md against actual tags in vault (run python3 wiki/scripts/index-tags.py --dry-run would be ideal, but since scripts don't have that flag yet, instead: run python3 wiki/scripts/index-tags.py, then check if any files in wiki/tags/ were deleted — if so, report them as 'stale tags previously unremoved'). 2) Missing descriptions: read wiki/tags/ files, report count of files without description frontmatter. 3) Broken wikilinks: spot-check wiki/tags/*.md — sample 10+ index files, verify each linked note exists in atoms/ or daily notes/. 4) Graph orphans: read wiki/data/graph-stats.md, report orphan count. 5) Artifact consistency: count tags in wiki/tags/ vs entries in wiki/tags-index.md, report drift. 6) Orphan MOC notes: read wiki/moc-index.md, check if hub notes reference files that don't exist. 7) Malformed links: spot-check atoms/ for links: frontmatter entries not using [[wikilink]] format. Report findings as a structured checklist. Do NOT modify any files. Log findings to wiki/LOG.md.

wiki-ingest.description: Suggest and apply tags + links for unprocessed notes (with #need-processing tag) in atoms/ or a specific note. Reads tag index, finds relevant tags and candidate links, presents a preview table, and applies only after user confirmation.
wiki-ingest.template: Load the llm-wiki skill. Step 1 — find target notes: if a filename is provided, use that single note; otherwise find all notes with the need-processing tag in atoms/ (max 10). Step 2 — for each note, read its content and frontmatter. Step 2a — find relevant tags: extract keywords from title, match against wiki/tags-index.md names and descriptions, exclude already-assigned and excluded tags, take top 5. Step 2b — find candidate links: for each relevant tag read wiki/tags/<tag>.md, grep vault for key phrases from title, exclude already-linked notes and self, score by common tags + keyword matches, take top 5. do NOT remove the need-processing tag. Step 3 — PREVIEW: show user a table with columns: Заметка, Текущие теги, Предлагаемые теги, Предлагаемые связи. WAIT for explicit user confirmation. Step 4 — only after user says 'yes' or 'да' or 'применить': update note frontmatter (merge tags and links, dedup), update wiki/tags/<tag>.md for each new tag, never remove existing data. Step 5 — log to wiki/LOG.md with ### Changes.
